hints.to hold back: That would be the infinitive of 'retinere'
hints.to live: That would be the infinitive of 'vivere'
hints.to worship: That would be the infinitive of 'adorare'

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'video' (to see)
hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the principal parts of 'teneo' (to hold)
hints.debeo, debere, debui, debitum: These are the principal parts of 'debeo' (to owe)

hints.nominative: The nominative plural would be 'dei' or 'dii'
hints.ablative: The ablative plural would be 'deis' or 'diis'
hints.vocative: The vocative plural would be 'dei' or 'dii'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns typically end in -a in the nominative singular
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension accusative plural would end in -es
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns end in -us (gen. -us) in the nominative

steps.reveal: coram me = before me / in my presence

hints.accusative: 'Me' can be accusative, but after 'coram' it is ablative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'ego/me' is an irregular personal pronoun
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'ego/me' is an irregular pronoun
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is genitive; 'ego/me' is an irregular pronoun

translation: You shall not have strange gods before me
